label: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support

node.label: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
node.definition: Tabular predictive systems estimate outcomes, risks, categories, or interventions from structured records using classical, probabilistic, and neural models.
node.why_it_matters: They remain the dominant practical boundary for credit, maintenance, operations, public services, and enterprise decision support.
limitations: Leakage, confounding, feedback loops, missing data, subgroup error, and changing business processes can invalidate results.

dependencies.rationale: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Linear models & generalized linear models as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates k-NN & Naive Bayes as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Support vector & kernel machines as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Gaussian processes as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Calibration, uncertainty & risk evaluation as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates PCA, ICA, NMF & manifold reduction as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Self-organizing maps & competitive learning as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis as part of an end-to-end application boundary.

sentence_units.text: Tabular predictive systems estimate outcomes, risks, categories, or interventions from structured records using classical, probabilistic, and neural models.
They remain the dominant practical boundary for credit, maintenance, operations, public services, and enterprise decision support.
Leakage, confounding, feedback loops, missing data, subgroup error, and changing business processes can invalidate results.
